description: baseline: accessibility validation not integrated into docs, critique model, or audit tooling

asi.hypothesis: Baseline should expose whether accessibility is currently a first-class success criterion in the showcase workflow.
asi.interpretation: There is contrast/design-system guidance but no explicit accessibility-tree/keyboard/accessible-name validation in the demo build loop.

description: integrate accessibility validation into showcase goals, routine prompt, critique rubric, and audit warnings

asi.hypothesis: Making accessibility an explicit criterion in the routine, manual critique workflow, typed rubric, and audit tool will close process gaps without touching existing demos yet.
asi.validation: deno fmt normalized docs; deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed. Audit now warns on likely static accessibility issues rather than failing current corpus.
asi.important_result: a11y_goal_gaps dropped from 7 to 0; current corpus still has 1801 static issues across 720 pages, so next work should reduce real demo issues rather than add more process text.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The new audit warning is intentionally not the whole accessibility definition; DevTools accessibility-tree and keyboard evidence remain required in docs/routine.

name: Chrome showcase static accessibility issue reduction

description: baseline for reducing objective static accessibility issues

asi.hypothesis: After accessibility process integration, the next useful target is reducing concrete static accessibility findings across demos.
baseline_sample: inputs may be missing labels in v130 part-state-machine and scope-truncation demos
cq-inspector has clickable non-control without role/tabindex
several nesting demos have potentially unlabeled textareas
asi.note: Primary metric changed intentionally from process integration gaps to corpus issue reduction.

description: add aria-labels to generated form controls with no programmatic label

asi.hypothesis: Most static accessibility findings are real unlabeled generated form controls. Adding aria-labels from existing placeholder/name/id values should improve semantics broadly without changing interaction behavior.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 1801 to 528. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed. Audit warning page count dropped from 720 to 162.
asi.risk: Large generated HTML touch count (1026 files, 2408 controls). Labels are derived mechanically, so follow-up spot checks with DevTools accessibility tree are still needed; some remaining inputs lack useful id/name/placeholder and need local context.
asi.next_action_hint: Address remaining categories: repeated decision-tree inputs with no useful label source, clickable non-controls, missing image alt, and icon-only buttons.

description: make inline clickable non-controls keyboard-operable and fix scanner attribute parsing

asi.hypothesis: Clickable div/span/etc. with inline onclick need keyboard activation and button semantics; the static scanner also needed to inspect the entire opening tag, not only attributes before onclick.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 528 to 341; audit warning page count dropped from 162 to 106. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, and deno task check passed.
asi.important_detail: Added role=button, tabindex=0, and Enter/Space onkeydown activation to 187 inline clickable non-controls across 58 files. Corrected measure/audit regex so attributes after onclick are counted.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining largest class is unlabeled inputs, especially decision-tree style demos lacking useful id/name/placeholder. Next inspect patterns before adding contextual labels.

description: repair malformed aria-label insertions from bulk control labelling

asi.hypothesis: Fixing malformed aria-label insertions caused by regex parsing of self-closing inputs and textarea placeholders containing > should improve correctness and may remove a small number of remaining findings.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues 341 -> 340; audit warning pages 106 -> 105. Checks passed and grep confirmed no remaining '/ aria-label=' or 'aria-label=""' artifacts.
asi.lesson: Bulk HTML edits with regex are risky when attribute values contain '>' or self-closing slash syntax. Future label passes should parse tags more defensively or target narrower patterns.

description: add accessible names to icon buttons and alt text to demo images

asi.hypothesis: Remaining unnamed buttons and images are small, targeted classes where accessible names/alt text can be added from local class/data context without changing behavior.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 340 to 294; audit warning pages dropped from 105 to 79. Checks and malformed-insertion grep passed.
asi.details: Added aria-label to 23 empty/icon buttons and alt to 26 img elements across 30 files. Heuristics: carousel dots become Go to slide N, selectedcontent buttons become Open picker, image maps/previews get descriptive alt, otherwise decorative/probe images get empty alt.
asi.risk: Some empty alt choices assume surrounding text explains decorative/probe images; browser accessibility-tree spot checks remain useful.

description: recognize form controls wrapped by label in static accessibility scanner

asi.hypothesis: Many remaining radio/checkbox findings are false positives because controls are wrapped by label elements; the scanner should recognize that native accessible-name pattern.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 294 to 3; audit warning pages dropped from 79 to 3. Checks passed.
asi.important_detail: This was a measurement correctness improvement, not a demo semantics change. It avoids pushing unnecessary aria-labels onto controls that already get names from wrapping labels.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the three remaining image alt findings manually by inspecting context.

asi.hypothesis: The last findings are two real markup issues plus JavaScript/code-sample scanner noise; fixing the markup and ignoring script/pre samples should clear the static audit without weakening real DOM checks.
asi.result: static_a11y_issues dropped from 3 to 0. deno fmt --check, deno check server.ts, deno task check, and malformed aria/alt grep all passed with no warnings.
changes: Percent-encoded the v133 image-map SVG data URI and kept a real outer alt value.
Moved v138 serialization demo aria-label out of the malicious sample value and onto the input itself.
Updated static scanner to ignore script blocks and pre/code examples so XSS payload strings are not treated as page DOM.
asi.anti_overfit_note: The scanner still checks actual page-shell DOM. It ignores examples/payload strings because those are deliberately displayed/generated content, not accessible DOM at load time.

description: document completed static accessibility reduction in playbook

asi.hypothesis: Updating the playbook with completed results helps future agents resume without rediscovering the same path, but should not count as a metric improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was already zero; no further metric improvement possible from documentation-only playbook update.
asi.next_action_hint: Next meaningful work is non-static validation: run representative demos through chrome-devtools-mcp accessibility tree and keyboard checks, or merge/push the completed branch after human review.

description: attempt DevTools accessibility-tree spot check after static audit reached zero

asi.hypothesis: With static accessibility findings at zero, the most valuable next validation is representative Chrome DevTools MCP spot-checking of keyboard behavior and accessibility tree semantics rather than changing more code.
asi.mcp_result: chrome-devtools-mcp connected/listed tools but page/list_pages calls timed out, so no browser or accessibility-tree claim was made.
asi.benchmark_result: Persisted benchmark remains green: static_a11y_issues=0, a11y_goal_gaps=0, audit_strict_errors=0.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and no primary-metric improvement; this was a validation attempt only.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry DevTools MCP accessibility-tree spot checks when the MCP server responds, starting with v138 serialization-diff and one page with added clickable non-control keyboard semantics. Do not merge solely on static audit results.
